article.title: Advice Needed !!
article.content:
Hi folks,

I am stuck in a situation right now. I recently underwent a heart surgery and I am recovering but I have resumed my work. 

I started giving some interviews and I am expecting an offer of around 20 LPA (All fixed) from a company (not a branded name but a decent one). The company is in Noida and I live in Gurgaon but the current company that I have been working is in Bangalore.

I have around 2 YOE. So, in a few months I can start trying and interviewing for SDE 2 roles.

I am stuck whether I should accept the new offer and join or should I wait for few months, prepare well and try for SDE 2 roles. If I accept this offer, I cannot leave before 1 year atleast because the interview was arranged through my friend's relative. If I leave before 1 year or so, it would look bad on them as they recommended a candidate who left early since they want someone who can remain for long term. Also, my location preference is Delhi NCR but I am open for Bangalore also. 

Also, due to my surgery, I am currently at my home doing WFH but if I resign I need to go to Bangalore for my notice period but I am not in a state to travel and manage alone there.

I am really confused what to do. Can some folks advice me like what should I do?

post.content: Share your conditions with your current organisation manager , HR and keep doing WFH till you get good recovery. I would say avoid joining new organisation because it can add stress of off-boarding and on-boarding. Currently your priority should be health. Pay, titles, tech-stack can wait. You have only 2 yoe, so have only touched eligibility for sde-2. Better to prepare and switch some months later. Keep in mind interviewing makes schedule hectic. Wishing you a speedy recovery.
Take some break to recover - it\'s the right decision on all perspective.
Should be easy to resign, just say your health is not good so would take a career break, would not be possible to move to Bangalore due to poor quality food. Also try to take a job that has good wlb.
